LAW OFFICES
NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

CENTER TOWER
650 TOWN CENTER DRIVE
SUITE 1250
COSTA MESA, CA 92626-1981

WALTER L. NOSSAMAN
(1886-1964)

LOS ANGELES
THIRTY-FIRST FLOOR
445 SOUTH FIGUEROA STREET
LOS ANGELES, CA 90071-1602
(213) 618-7800

SAN FRANCISCO
THIRTY-FOURTH FLOOR
50 CALIFORNIA STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111-4712
(418) 398-3600

TELEPHONE (714) 838-9052
FACSIMILE (714) 545-0838

WARREN ELLIOTT
OF COUNSEL

WASHINGTON, D.C.
SUITE 800
1275 PENNSYLVANIA AVE., N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20037-1156
(202) 223-9100

SACRAMENTO
SUITE 1000
915 L STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814-3701
(916) 442-8888

February 19, 1991

WRITER'S DIRECT DIAL NUMBER                    REFER TO FILE NUMBER


John W. Wallace, Counsel
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices
Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

     Re:  Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Wallace:

     This firm is general counsel to the San Joaquin Hills
Transportation  Corridor  Agency  and  the  Foothill/Eastern
Transportation Corridor Agency.  Mr. John B. Shaw is the City
Attorney of the City of San Juan Capistrano.  This letter is
submitted jointly on behalf of the City of San Juan Capistrano
("City"), the Foothill/Eastern Transportation Corridor Agency
("FE/ETCA") and the San Joaquin Hills Transportation Corridor
Agency ("SJHTCA") with regard to the participation of San Juan
City Councilman Gary Hausdorfer in decisions concerning the San
Joaquin Hills Transportation Corridor and Foothill/Eastern
Transportation Corridors.  It is follow-up to our telephone
conversation with you on February 1, 1991 regarding this
matter.  We request written advice of the legal counsel to the
California Fair Political Practices Commission regarding the
duties and responsibilities of Mr. Hausdorfer under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

     This request is based on the following facts:

     1.  Mr. Hausdorfer is a member of the City Council of
the City of San Juan Capistrano.  He is the appointed

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT

John W. Wallace, Counsel
February 19, 1991
Page 2

representative of the City to the Board of Directors of the
F/ETCA and is the appointed alternate member of the City to the
Board of Directors of the SJHTCA.

2. The F/ETCA and the SJHTCA are joint powers
agencies formed by the County of Orange, the City and a number
of other cities within the County. The F/ETCA is authorized to
plan, design, finance and construct the Foothill and Eastern
Transportation Corridors which are proposed tollways in the
County. The SJHTCA is authorized to plan, design, finance and
construct the San Joaquin Hills Transportation Corridor within
the County of Orange.

3. The City is a party to the joint exercise of
powers agreements forming the Foothill/Eastern Transportation
Corridor Agency and the San Joaquin Hills Transportation
Corridor Agency. Periodically, the City Council of the City
considers corridor issues affecting the City in order to make
recommendations to the City's appointed member of the Boards of
Directors of the F/ETCA and the SJHTCA.

4. Since October 1988, Mr. Hausdorfer's spouse has
been employed by the Keith Companies. The Keith Companies is a
planning and engineering firm which has offices within the
County of Orange. The Keith Companies has a contract with the
SJHTCA to prepare an Environmental Impact Report for the
realignment of a local arterial within the City of Newport
Beach which connects to the San Joaquin Hills Corridor. The
work on this contract is scheduled to be completed in June
1991. Mr. Hausdorfer did not participate in any of the
decisions of the Board of Directors of the SJHTC regarding the
foregoing contract with the Keith Companies.

5. The Keith Companies has also entered into a
contract with the F/ETCA to prepare an engineering design for a
section of the Foothill Transportation Corridor. The work on
this contract is scheduled to be completed in March 1991. Mr.
Hausdorfer did not participate in any of the decisions of the
Board of Directors of the F/ETCA regarding the contract with
the Keith Companies.

6. The F/ETCA and the SJHTCA have entered into
approximately 120 planning, engineering and other consulting
contracts regarding the three Corridors. The dollar value of
the two contracts with the Keith Companies referred to above
represents less than one percent of the dollar value of the
contracts entered into by the SJHTCA and the F/ETCA to date.
The Keith Companies is not on the pre-qualified list of

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT

John W. Wallace, Counsel
February 19, 1991
Page 3

engineering and construction firms which have been invited to
bid on the design and construction of the San Joaquin and
Eastern Corridors. While a very significant percentage of the
planning and engineering work for the three corridors has been
completed, additional professional service contracts will be
entered into by the F/ETCA and the SJHTCA. Because of the
large number of firms which are capable of providing services,
it is not possible to know whether or not the Kelth Companies
will be requested to respond, or invited to bid, by the F/ETCA
or the SJHTCA regarding any other future professional service
contracts, or if invited to bid, whether they would be awarded
any such contract.

In light of the foregoing facts, we request the advice
of your office regarding the following issues:

(1) May Mr. Hausdorfer participate in the
consideration of Transportation Corridor related
issues by the City Council of the City of San Juan
Capistrano?

(2) May Mr. Hausdorfer participate in decisions of
the F/ETCA and the SJHTCA concerning the approval of
the Corridor projects?

Very truly yours,

Robert D. Thornton
of NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX
& ELLIOTT

John R. Shaw
City Attorney
City of San Juan Capistrano

cc: John R. Shaw, Esq.
RDT:lmb
